entries.description: Senate Select Committee on Intelligence Torture Report: CIA ran secret black sites and used 'enhanced interrogation' (torture) on 119 detainees including waterboarding, rectal feeding, sleep deprivation; program was ineffective and CIA misled Congress, White House, and DOJ
entries.causation_evidence: 6,700-page Senate Intelligence Committee report (declassified executive summary Dec 2014)

entries.description: CIA destroyed 92 videotapes of torture sessions at black sites (waterboarding of Abu Zubaydah and others) despite court orders and internal objections
entries.causation_evidence: Senate Torture Report and CIA Inspector General findings

entries.description: Extraordinary rendition program: CIA secretly transferred detainees to foreign countries known for torture (e.g., Egypt, Syria, Libya) for proxy interrogation
entries.causation_evidence: Senate Torture Report and Open Society Foundations global rendition study

entries.description: MKUltra mind-control experiments: CIA conducted illegal LSD and hypnosis tests on unwitting U.S. and Canadian citizens (including prisoners and mental patients) for 20+ years
entries.causation_evidence: Church Committee report and 1977 declassified MKUltra documents

entries.description: Church Committee findings: CIA conducted illegal domestic surveillance, assassination plots (Castro, Lumumba), mail opening, and media manipulation (Operation Mockingbird)
Afghanistan intelligence failure: CIA underestimated Taliban collapse speed, warned White House of rapid takeover but assessments were ignored or downplayed
entries.causation_evidence: Senate Foreign Relations Committee and former CIA counterterrorism chief statements

entries.description: Kabul drone strike: CIA/Pentagon Hellfire missile killed 10 civilians (including 7 children) from one family instead of ISIS-K target
entries.causation_evidence: Pentagon investigation and New York Times visual reconstruction

entries.description: Secret CIA bulk surveillance program: unauthorized mass collection of Americans' data under Executive Order 12333, with poor handling and privacy violations
entries.causation_evidence: Privacy and Civil Liberties Oversight Board report (declassified 2022)

entries.description: CIA retracted 19 intelligence products (including DEI-related assessments on extremism and pandemic impacts) after President's Intelligence Advisory Board found political bias and poor sourcing
entries.causation_evidence: CIA Director Ratcliffe statement and released redacted reports (Feb 2026)

entries.description: Iran-Contra affair: CIA facilitated illegal arms sales to Iran and diverted proceeds to fund Nicaraguan Contras in violation of Boland Amendment
entries.causation_evidence: Tower Commission and congressional Iran-Contra reports

entries.description: Phoenix Program (Vietnam): CIA-run assassination and torture program targeting suspected Viet Cong, resulting in thousands of civilian deaths and abuses
entries.causation_evidence: Church Committee and Pentagon Papers references

entries.description: Chronic drone strike intelligence failures: high civilian casualty rates in Yemen, Somalia, and Afghanistan due to poor targeting data and over-reliance on signals intelligence
entries.causation_evidence: Intercept 'Drone Papers' and Bureau of Investigative Journalism tracking

entries.description: Operation Cyclone blowback: CIA funding of Afghan mujahideen (including future al-Qaeda elements) contributed to long-term regional instability
entries.causation_evidence: Congressional records and declassified CIA documents

entries.description: Operation Neptune Spear: CIA intelligence operation that located and enabled the killing of Osama bin Laden
entries.causation_evidence: Official White House and CIA statements

entries.description: 2026 analytic objectivity reforms: Director Ratcliffe ordered retraction/revision of 19 biased products and reinforced tradecraft standards
entries.causation_evidence: CIA official press release and PIAB review

entries.description: Ongoing failure to fully declassify Senate Torture Report: full 6,700-page document remains classified more than 10 years after completion
